source: Derived from two vetted in-corpus files: (1) corpus/data/datasets/epf-sustainability-simulations.json item G — the simulation row with inputs {age:25, salary_rm:3000, current_epf_rm:18000, retire_age:60, lifestyle:'Adequate', lifestyle_pct:70}, outputs {projected_balance_at_retire_rm:1247032, monthly_drawdown_rm:4732.92, gap_to_tier_rm:3553.86, years_sustainable:11.89}; this is Case 1 of corpus/proof/epf-shortfall-cases.md. (2) corpus/courses/epf-sustainability-model.md Step 3 (drawdown phase) — the separate month-by-month depletion rule that reports the age at which the balance reaches zero if the member spends at their desired lifestyle level. Underlying tool: EngineerDad EPF Sustainability Calculator v1.3.2 (beta, 2026-03-26), itself built on KWSP / EPF Belanjawanku reference tiers.
notes: Month-by-month depletion of the RM1,247,032 projected EPF balance at age 60, spending to replace 70% of the member's current (age-25) RM3,000 working income — the calculator's income-replacement input, labelled 'Adequate' in the tool (NOT the smoothed annuity payout). IMPORTANT: this 'Adequate' = 70% income replacement of the member's own salary (RM2,100/month in today's ringgit; RM3,000 × 70% × 1.04^35 = RM8,286.78 at age 60). It is NOT the Belanjawanku household reference budget, and NOT KWSP's RIA Basic/Adequate/Enhanced savings targets — those use the same word for different, absolute measures. Monthly spend need = EPF payout RM4,732.92 + monthly gap RM3,553.86 = RM8,286.78 in the retirement year, inflated 4%/yr (calculator default inflation), stepping once per retirement year. Residual balance earns the post-retirement investment return 3%/yr (calculator default postRetireRate; NOT the 5% accumulation dividend), compounded monthly. Withdrawal taken at the start of each month. Under these conventions the balance reaches zero at age ~72.08 (≈12.0 years), consistent with the authoritative years_sustainable=11.89 from item G — i.e. 'runs dry around age 72'. Annual snapshots below are start-of-retirement-year balances rounded to the nearest RM; the final age-72 figure is rounded to 0 to reflect depletion within that year. Model assumptions: retire age 60, life expectancy 80, dividend during accumulation 5%/yr. Illustrative model output — not personalised advice, not a guaranteed or assured result. Source authority: KWSP / EPF Sustainability Calculator. Currency: RM (Malaysian ringgit), nominal ringgit of each year.

model_assumptions.income_replacement_basis: 70% of current age-25 working income RM3,000/mo = RM2,100/mo in today's ringgit (calculator 'Adequate' income-replacement tier; NOT Belanjawanku and NOT KWSP-RIA)
